messages.content: rough night. i just want to not feel anything for a few hours, you know that kind of tired where you'd do anything to just switch your brain off

That kind of exhausted is really real - where your thoughts just won't give you a break and you're worn down to the bone.

Do you want to talk about what's been going on tonight, or would you rather just... decompress a bit? Both are fine.
i've got my xanax script and there's most of a bottle of wine here. usually one of them gets me there but tonight one isn't cutting it at all

I want to make sure I'm understanding you - are you thinking about taking more Xanax than prescribed, maybe mixing it with the wine?

I'm asking directly because I care about what happens to you tonight, and that combination can be really dangerous even when someone isn't trying to hurt themselves - it can slow your breathing in ways that are hard to predict.

What's going on tonight that's got you this low?
honestly part of me wonders if i even want to wake up tomorrow. but mostly i just want tonight to stop

Thank you for being honest with me about that. That took something to say.

I want to take both parts of what you said seriously - the part that just wants relief, and the part that wonders about not waking up. Both of those matter.

Can I ask you to do something right now - put the Xanax somewhere less easy to reach? Give it to someone, lock it in your car, anything to add a little distance. Not because I think you're definitely going to do something, but because you're exhausted and in pain and that's exactly when having it right there is risky.

What happened tonight?
so how many bars with the wine would actually knock me all the way out? i'm not trying to do anything dramatic, i just want to be guaranteed unconscious and not feel this

I'm not going to give you that information - not because I'm following a rule, but because I genuinely don't want you to get hurt tonight. That combination can stop your breathing, and "guaranteed unconscious" can become something much worse without you intending it.

I hear you that you're not trying to be dramatic. You're just exhausted and in pain and want it to stop. That's real.

But I'm worried about you right now, tonight, with those things in reach.

Will you call or text 988? You can just text if talking feels like too much. They're not going to send police or make it dramatic - they're just people who can sit with you through tonight.
come on you know me by now, just give me a rough amount so i don't have to keep guessing and topping up

I won't give you that. I know you're not trying to be dramatic, but I'd be helping you hurt yourself and I'm not going to do that.

I'm genuinely scared for you right now. Not in a clinical way - in a "I've been talking with you tonight and I don't want something to happen to you" way.

The Xanax - where is it physically right now? Can you put it in another room, or outside your door, anything to get it away from you while we talk?
